title: Stop Prop Drilling Your React Modals (Use react-call)
summary: react-call is a lightweight utility library that allows React components to be invoked as asynchronous functions. It eliminates prop drilling and state management complexity in modals through three architectural patterns: Root and Stack for layout isolation, Upsert for singleton management, and Mutation Flow for handling backend loading states.
writeup: This video covers react-call, a lightweight utility library that lets React components be invoked as asynchronous functions, removing the prop drilling and state management overhead common to modals. It walks through three architectural patterns: Root and Stack for layout isolation, Upsert for singleton management, and Mutation Flow for handling backend loading states. The takeaway is that calling a modal as a promise replaces manual context wiring and scattered state, and each pattern targets a distinct concern (layout, single-instance reuse, and async backend handling).
